merge.diff:
diff --git a/CHANGES.rst b/CHANGES.rst
index 88e167d2..7dfd6062 100644
--- a/CHANGES.rst
+++ b/CHANGES.rst
@@ -36,6 +36,8 @@ Unreleased
 -   The ``groupby`` filter is case-insensitive by default, matching
     other comparison filters. Added the ``case_sensitive`` parameter to
     control this. :issue:`1463`
+-   ``FileSystemLoader`` expands environment variables and ``~user``
+    home directory shortcuts in search paths.
 
 
 Version 3.0.3
diff --git a/src/jinja2/loaders.py b/src/jinja2/loaders.py
index d7d9bd04..131f8e70 100644
--- a/src/jinja2/loaders.py
+++ b/src/jinja2/loaders.py
@@ -38,6 +38,18 @@ def split_template_path(template: str) -> t.List[str]:
     return pieces
 
 
+def _transform_path(
+    path_transform: t.Optional[t.Callable[[str], str]], template: str
+) -> str:
+    """Apply an optional path transformation function to a template
+    path.  Returns ``template`` unchanged if ``path_transform`` is
+    ``None``.
+    """
+    if path_transform is None:
+        return template
+    return path_transform(template)
+
+
 class BaseLoader:
     """Baseclass for all loaders.  Subclass this and override `get_source` to
     implement a custom loading mechanism.  The environment provides a
@@ -166,13 +178,26 @@ class FileSystemLoader(BaseLoader):
         loader = FileSystemLoader(["/override/templates", "/default/templates"])
 
     :param searchpath: A path, or list of paths, to the directory that
-        contains the templates.
+        contains the templates. Environment variables (``$VAR`` or
+        ``${VAR}``) and a leading ``~`` or ``~user`` are expanded.
     :param encoding: Use this encoding to read the text from template
         files.
     :param followlinks: Follow symbolic links in the path.
+    :param path_transform: An optional function that takes the
+        requested template path and returns a transformed path to use
+        for resolution instead. Useful for template aliasing or path
+        normalization. Defaults to ``None`` which disables
+        transformation.
+
+    .. versionchanged:: 3.1
+        Search paths now have environment variables and ``~user``
+        expanded.
 
     .. versionchanged:: 2.8
         Added the ``followlinks`` parameter.
+
+    .. versionadded:: 3.1
+        Added the ``path_transform`` parameter.
     """
 
     def __init__(
@@ -180,18 +205,22 @@ class FileSystemLoader(BaseLoader):
         searchpath: t.Union[str, os.PathLike, t.Sequence[t.Union[str, os.PathLike]]],
         encoding: str = "utf-8",
         followlinks: bool = False,
+        path_transform: t.Optional[t.Callable[[str], str]] = None,
     ) -> None:
         if not isinstance(searchpath, abc.Iterable) or isinstance(searchpath, str):
             searchpath = [searchpath]
 
-        self.searchpath = [os.fspath(p) for p in searchpath]
+        self.searchpath = [
+            os.path.expanduser(os.path.expandvars(os.fspath(p))) for p in searchpath
+        ]
         self.encoding = encoding
         self.followlinks = followlinks
+        self.path_transform = path_transform
 
     def get_source(
         self, environment: "Environment", template: str
     ) -> t.Tuple[str, str, t.Callable[[], bool]]:
-        pieces = split_template_path(template)
+        pieces = split_template_path(_transform_path(self.path_transform, template))
         for searchpath in self.searchpath:
             filename = os.path.join(searchpath, *pieces)
             f = open_if_exists(filename)
@@ -239,6 +268,10 @@ class PackageLoader(BaseLoader):
     :param package_path: Directory within the imported package that
         contains the templates.
     :param encoding: Encoding of template files.
+    :param path_transform: An optional function that takes the
+        requested template path and returns a transformed path to use
+        for resolution instead. Defaults to ``None`` which disables
+        transformation.
 
     The following example looks up templates in the ``pages`` directory
     within the ``project.ui`` package.
@@ -262,6 +295,9 @@ class PackageLoader(BaseLoader):
 
     .. versionchanged:: 3.0
         Limited PEP 420 namespace package support.
+
+    .. versionadded:: 3.1
+        Added the ``path_transform`` parameter.
     """
 
     def __init__(
@@ -269,6 +305,7 @@ class PackageLoader(BaseLoader):
         package_name: str,
         package_path: "str" = "templates",
         encoding: str = "utf-8",
+        path_transform: t.Optional[t.Callable[[str], str]] = None,
     ) -> None:
         package_path = os.path.normpath(package_path).rstrip(os.path.sep)
 
@@ -281,6 +318,7 @@ class PackageLoader(BaseLoader):
         self.package_path = package_path
         self.package_name = package_name
         self.encoding = encoding
+        self.path_transform = path_transform
 
         # Make sure the package exists. This also makes namespace
         # packages work, otherwise get_loader returns None.
@@ -326,7 +364,10 @@ class PackageLoader(BaseLoader)
